title: Mailchimp vs GetResponse (2025): Which Is Better?
meta_description: Compare Mailchimp and GetResponse email marketing platforms. See pricing, features, and which tool suits your business needs.
h1: Mailchimp vs GetResponse: Complete Comparison Guide
intro: Both Mailchimp and GetResponse are established email marketing platforms offering freemium models and robust automation capabilities. While Mailchimp excels at serving beginners and small businesses with its intuitive interface, GetResponse targets online educators and webinar hosts with specialized features. Your choice depends on your specific marketing goals and the features you prioritize.
choose_a_if: You're a beginner or small business owner seeking an easy-to-use platform
You want the lowest entry price point for paid features
You need strong drag-and-drop email design tools without complexity
choose_b_if: You host webinars or create online courses as part of your marketing
You need advanced conversion funnel building capabilities
You want better customer support ratings and reviews

faq.a: It depends on your needs. Mailchimp is better for beginners and small businesses due to its simpler interface and lower pricing, with a G2 rating of 4.3. However, GetResponse offers more specialized features for webinar hosts and course creators, backed by stronger customer satisfaction (3.7 Trustpilot rating).

faq.q: Which is cheaper, Mailchimp or GetResponse?
faq.a: Mailchimp is the cheaper option, starting at $13/month compared to GetResponse's $19/month. Both offer identical free tiers with 500 subscriber limits, making Mailchimp the more budget-friendly choice for businesses scaling up from free plans.

faq.q: Can I switch from GetResponse to Mailchimp?
faq.a: Yes, both platforms support email list exports, allowing you to migrate your contacts relatively easily. Most email marketing platforms provide CSV export options, though you may need to manually reconfigure automations and templates in your new platform.
